sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Left is selected. Let me now look at the g markers and their positions relative to the crosses.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Inspect g and G node positions
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('G', 'g', 'N'):
        b = n['boundary']
        min_row = min(r for r, c in b)
        max_row = max(r for r, c in b)
        min_col = min(c for r, c in b)
        max_col = max(c for r, c in b)
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min_row},{min_col})-({max_row},{max_col})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the g markers more clearly:
- Node 13: G frame around Left cross at (17,16)-(22,21)
- Node 15: Left cross N cells at (18,17)-(21,20)
- Node 17: Middle cross N cells at (18,23)-(21,26)
- Node 20: g at (18,30)-(18,31) - this is to the RIGHT of Middle cross
- Node 23: Right cross N cells at (18,35)-(21,38)
- Node 27: g at (19,29)-(20,29) - this is to the RIGHT of Middle cross
- Node 29: g at (19,32)-(20,32) - this is to the LEFT of Right cross
- Node 33: g at (21,30)-(21,31) - this is to the RIGHT of Middle cross
So the g markers are:
1. On the RIGHT side of Middle cross (cols 29-31, rows 18-21)
2. On the LEFT side of Right cross (cols 32, rows 19-20)
This is interesting! The g markers are NOT on the Middle cross itself, but on the cells BETWEEN Middle and Right crosses. They're on the side of the gap between Middle and Right.
Wait, but I said earlier that Left → Right was invalid (reset). So why are the g markers pointing towards Right?
Hmm, let me reconsider. Maybe the g markers don't indicate valid next steps. Maybe they indicate something else, like the direction of movement or a path that was taken.
Actually, wait. Let me look at this more carefully. When Left is selected:
- g markers appear between Middle and Right crosses
- This is NOT between Left and Middle
So the g markers are NOT pointing to the previously-clicked cross. They're pointing to a completely different direction!
Let me reconsider. When I click on Left:
- G frame appears around Left
- g markers appear between Middle and Right
When I click on Middle:
- G frame appears around Middle
- g markers appear between Left and Middle (on the LEFT side of Middle)
Wait, that doesn't match. Let me check the previous state when Middle was selected.
Actually, I need to re-examine. Let me look at the ascii output from when Middle was selected:
Row 18: ...WwwwwWGGNNGGWwNNwWWwggwWWwNNwWWwwwwWWwwwwWBb...
Hmm, I don't have the exact ascii from when Middle was selected. Let me try clicking on Middle and examining the g positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on Middle and inspect g positions
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 24}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
